pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 11 and export_result.sh line 7 both reference this exact path consistently
Task description (lines 1-5 of task.json) specifies 'plate_width: 80 mm'. This is a standard dimension for mounting plates. Width of 80mm is typical for small brackets/enclosure mounts.
Task description specifies 'plate_height: 50 mm'. Standard aspect ratio (80:50 = 1.6:1) for landscape-oriented mounting plates.
Task description specifies 'plate_thickness: 3 mm'. 3mm is standard sheet metal thickness in metric series (1.5, 2, 2.5, 3, 4, 5mm). Matches fillet radius - good design practice.
Task description specifies 'hole_diameter: 4 mm'. Standard clearance hole size for M3 screws. Calculation: volume per hole = π × 2² × 3 ≈ 37.7 mm³
Task description specifies 'hole_edge_offset: 6 mm'. Verified as reasonable for 4-corner mounting pattern: 50mm height / 2 - 6mm = 19mm from center, leaving 38mm center-to-center spacing.
Task description specifies 'corner_fillet: 3 mm'. Equals plate thickness - excellent design choice to avoid stress concentration. Typical manufacturing constraint: fillet_radius ≤ thickness.
Calculated from parameters: Base box (80×50×3=12000) - 4 holes (4×π×2²×3≈151) - fillets (~300-400mm³) ≈ 11450-11649 mm³. Metadata range 11500-12100 encompasses calculated range with margin.

Conservative upper bound that accounts for FreeCAD's geometric calculation variance. Base volume is 12000 mm³; range 12100 allows for minimal fillet effects or rounding. Reasonable engineering tolerance.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic CAD task. No external data sources exist - geometry is generated from scratch in FreeCAD. All parameter values are verified against task specification: plate dimensions (80×50×3 mm) are standard engineering sizes; hole diameter (4mm) is a standard clearance hole for M3 fasteners; offset (6mm) creates valid 4-corner mounting pattern with 38mm center-to-center spacing; fillet radius (3mm) equals thickness, following good design practice. Expected volume range (11500-12100 mm³) is correctly calculated: base box (12000 mm³) minus 4 cylindrical holes (≈151 mm³) minus corner fillets (≈300-350 mm³) ≈ 11550 mm³, centered within metadata bounds. All design parameters are internally consistent and follow standard mechanical engineering conventions.
